steps.title: Link anatomy to detection
steps.content: The team's conclusion is about anatomical features affecting dogs' ability to distinguish speech from nonspeech. Supporting evidence must compare dogs with different head shapes and show a difference between responses to real language and scrambled nonspeech.

steps.title: Check the study findings
steps.content:
• Choice A: Compares different groups under different conditions, so it does not isolate the speech-nonspeech distinction.
  • Choice B: Links longer heads to a larger brain-activity gap between language and scrambled sound.
  • Choice C: Compares familiar and unfamiliar languages, both speech.
  • Choice D: Compares long-headed dogs by language background, not anatomy or nonspeech.
The answer is B.
